title: Commodities Brief — 08 Jul 2026
intro:
Energy and agriculture lead gains amid Middle East supply concerns and adverse weather impacting US crop forecasts. Precious metals consolidate near record highs as markets digest recent strength while base metals show resilience on infrastructure demand.
🥇 PRECIOUS METALS
Gold $4133.90 -0.28% intraday | Bias: NEUTRAL-LONG | Key: $4100/$4180. Holding above psychological $4100 support after recent rally, consolidation healthy ahead of next leg. Silver $60.79 -0.22% tracking gold with industrial demand supporting at $60 floor. Platinum $1644.90 -0.38% strongest performer this week +1.75%, automotive sector recovery and supply deficits from South Africa underpinning. Palladium $1269.50 steady, supply tightness offsetting substitution concerns.

sections.heading: 🛢️ COMMODITIES SUMMARY
sections.content:
Energy and agriculture lead gains amid Middle East supply concerns and adverse weather impacting US crop forecasts. Precious metals consolidate near record highs as markets digest recent strength while base metals show resilience on infrastructure demand.
🥇 PRECIOUS METALS
Gold $4133.90 -0.28% intraday | Bias: NEUTRAL-LONG | Key: $4100/$4180. Holding above psychological $4100 support after recent rally, consolidation healthy ahead of next leg. Silver $60.79 -0.22% tracking gold with industrial demand supporting at $60 floor. Platinum $1644.90 -0.38% strongest performer this week +1.75%, automotive sector recovery and supply deficits from South Africa underpinning. Palladium $1269.50 steady, supply tightness offsetting substitution concerns.

sections.heading: ⚡ ENERGY
sections.content:
WTI $72.73 +3.25% intraday | Bias: LONG | Key: $70/$76. Sharp rally on reports of pipeline disruption in Iraq's southern export terminals, supply concerns overriding demand weakness. Brent $76.57 +3.25% mirroring WTI strength, weekly gains above 6% signal momentum shift. Brent-WTI spread $3.84 normalizing as US export capacity tightens. Natural Gas $3.28 +0.43%, modest gains on cooling demand forecasts for mid-July heatwave.
🌾 AGRICULTURE
Corn $463.00 +4.63% leading grains on USDA crop condition downgrades, excessive heat across Midwest reducing yield potential. Wheat $621.25 +1.97% supported by Black Sea export uncertainty and US spring wheat concerns. Soybeans $1199.25 +0.21% lagging but firmer on spillover demand and South American production cuts.

sections.heading: 🎯 TRADE IDEAS
sections.content:
LONG: WTI Crude - geopolitical premium building - entry $72.50 - SL $69.80 - TP $78.00
LONG: Corn - weather deterioration accelerating - entry $462 - SL $445 - TP $490
SHORT: Gold - overextended near $4150 resistance - entry $4145 - SL $4185 - TP $4070

sections.heading: ⚠️ RISKS
sections.content:
Fed pivoting hawkish on sticky inflation could trigger broad commodity selloff and dollar strength.
Rapid de-escalation in Middle East tensions would reverse energy risk premium and pressure oil $8-12 lower.
